description_long: This is a shallow, round ceramic bowl with a wide rim and a gently sloping interior. The bowl features intricate hand-drawn decorative motifs, including a fish with detailed scales and a border of repeating spiral patterns along the inner rim. The surface shows visible signs of age and restoration, with several areas where the original glaze and decoration are missing or have been reconstructed. The coloration is primarily a pale beige or off-white, with brown and dark greenish lines forming the decorative elements. The bowl's exterior is less decorated, with some areas showing wear and discoloration. The overall appearance suggests it is an antique or archaeological artifact, likely used for serving or display.
description: A shallow, round ceramic bowl with hand-drawn fish and spiral motifs, showing signs of age and restoration. The surface is mostly beige with brown and greenish decorative lines.
description_view_0: This view highlights the fish motif and the spiral border along the rim, with visible restoration patches.
description_view_1: This angle shows more of the interior decoration, including additional abstract motifs and the continuation of the spiral border.
description_view_2: This side reveals the most worn and restored areas, with less visible decoration and more plain surface.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the opposite side of the bowl, showing the spiral border and some abstract motifs, with restoration evident.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Hand-drawn fish motif and spiral border pattern on the interior rim, with additional abstract motifs.
